threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it the 'Overground' instead of just the 'Ginger Line'. Also, your taste in street food hasn’t yet leveled up to Berber & Q.
Last night on the 36, a bloke serenaded everyone with a ukulele. Felt like being in a scratched-up indie film. Only in Peckham.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a hipster barista argue with a Turkish baker over who makes the best lahmacun was when I knew this place had heart.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you grab a vegan döner at 2 AM and be serenaded by a saxophonist on the corner. It’s a late-night ritual you won’t find anywhere else.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they roll their eyes at the BART delays but still give the train operators a nod of understanding. It’s a love-hate thing.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 19 Polk this morning; a guy serenading us with a ukulele. Guess the Muni magic still lives on in unexpected ways.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists assume every bar serves tapas, but locals know some only offer pintxos. Exploring the hidden gems is what makes Gràcia special.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their struggle to navigate the narrow streets, pausing at every corner to consult Google Maps while others weave past effortlessly.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You call it 'the Overground,' but the locals just say 'the train.' Plus, good luck knowing the difference between Fish Island and Fish Street!
Last night on the 35, a guy started singing 'Sweet Caroline' and everyone joined in like it was a karaoke bar. Only in Brixton, right?

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a guy fixing his bike while juggling two beers, and the whole park just cheered him on. Only in Neukölln do you find that kind of spirit.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg do people queue for currywurst at 4 AM on a Tuesday while debating the best late-night falafel spot. It's a culinary marathon.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You'll spot a local if they know the secret shortcut through the alley behind Dolores Park to avoid the 48-hour BART delays. No tourists take that route.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni bus crammed with people, everyone's glued to their phones, one gal kept playing her ukulele. You can't make this stuff up.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all locals hang out at parks; actually, most avoid the crowds and prefer the hidden plazas to sip coffee quietly.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their hesitation at the T-10 card machine—those puzzled expressions are classic. Locals glide right through.
